title: Mold — discard starter
warnings: Mold is a food safety issue.
dontDo: do not remove only top layer

title: Mold — restart
steps: Start with small flour and water portion.
dontDo: do not bake with moldy starter

title: Rotten smell — discard
summary: Rotten or musty smell means stop.
warnings: Sour smell may be normal; rotten smell is not.
dontDo: do not hide smell by feeding

title: Rotten smell — restart
warnings: Safety is more important than saving flour.

title: Starter not rising — plan
steps: Add 20 g water and 20 g flour.
warnings: Mold or rotten smell means discard.
dontDo: do not bake with inactive starter

title: Dough not rising — plan
summary: Correct with time, temperature and active starter.
steps: Next time use more active starter.
warnings: Do not fix by aggressive heating.
dontDo: do not judge only by clock

title: Dense crumb
summary: Check fermentation and whether bread cooled.

title: Dense crumb — correction
steps: Reduce water if dough was too wet.
warnings: One failure does not mean bad starter.
dontDo: do not change everything at once

title: Pale crust
summary: Check oven temperature and bake time.
warnings: Do not dry the inside endlessly.

title: Pale crust — plan
steps: Use steam or cover if safe.
warnings: Be careful with steam and hot pot.
dontDo: do not pour water on unsafe glass

title: Bread cracking
summary: May need scoring, longer proof or less dry surface.
dontDo: do not bake tight loaf without scoring

title: Bread cracking — plan
summary: Correct scoring, proof and surface moisture.
steps: Use steam or pot if safe.
dontDo: do not score with dull knife

title: Feeding schedule
summary: Feed a small starter portion regularly.
steps: Add equal weights flour and water.

title: Feeding schedule — plan
summary: Plan for active and fridge starter.
steps: Feed before baking and wait for rise.
warnings: Mold or rotten smell means discard.

title: Basic diagnosis
summary: Write problem, time, temperature and smell.
dontDo: do not fix everything at once

title: Universal correction plan
summary: Improve next bake one change at a time.
warnings: Home guidance, not full food-safety assessment.
